question_types.title: Midpoints and perpendicular bisectors 6A — Midpoint Calculations

questions.question: Find the midpoint of the line segment joining (4, 2) and (6, 8).
Find the midpoint of the line segment joining (0, 6) and (12, 2).

question_types.title: Midpoints and perpendicular bisectors 6A — Solving for Unknown Endpoints

questions.question: The line segment AB has A(-2, 5) and midpoint M(4, 3). Find B.

question_types.title: Midpoints and perpendicular bisectors 6B — Perpendicular Bisector Equations

questions.question: Find the perpendicular bisector of the segment joining A(-5, 8) and B(7, 2).
questions.explanation: Midpoint is (1, 5). Gradient of AB = (2-8)/(7-(-5)) = -6/12 = -1/2. Perpendicular gradient = 2. y - 5 = 2(x - 1) => y = 2x + 3.

question_types.title: Equation of a circle 6C — Centre and Radius from Expanded Form

questions.question: Find the centre and radius of the circle: x² + y² - 2x + 8y - 8 = 0.

question_types.title: Equation of a circle 6C — Circle Equation from Diameter

questions.question: PQ is a diameter of a circle where P = (5, 6) and Q = (-2, 2). Find the equation of the circle.

question_types.title: Intersections of straight lines and circles 6D — Intersections with Axes

questions.question: Find the coordinates of the points where (x - 1)² + (y - 3)² = 45 meets the x-axis.

question_types.title: Intersections of straight lines and circles 6D — Proving Non-Intersection

questions.question: Show that the line x - y - 10 = 0 does not meet the circle x² - 4x + y² = 21.
questions.explanation: Substitute y = x - 10: x² - 4x + (x-10)² = 21 => 2x² - 24x + 79 = 0. Discriminant = 576 - 632 = -56 < 0. No intersection.

question_types.title: Use tangent and chord properties 6E — Tangent Equations

questions.question: The point P(1, -2) lies on the circle with centre (4, 6). Find the equation of the tangent to the circle at P.

question_types.title: Circles and triangles 6F — Right-angled Triangles and Diameters

questions.question: Points A(2, 6), B(5, 7) and C(8, -2) lie on a circle. Show that AC is a diameter.
questions.explanation: mAB = (7-6)/(5-2) = 1/3. mBC = (-2-7)/(8-5) = -3. (1/3) × (-3) = -1. Angle in semicircle is 90°, so AC is the diameter.
options: mAB × mBC = -1, so angle ABC = 90°, making AC a diameter.
All three points are equidistant from the midpoint of BC
The length AC equals the sum AB + BC
